english: scruple, doubt, uncertainty, hesitation, occasionally used for te-tsóm (the tshom).

C. also *ti-ba* (*thi ba*), pf. tes (thes) Sch., the col. syn. of ytógs-pa (gtogs pa), seldom in B., 1. to belong, appertain to, c. la. — 2. to occupy one's self with a thing, to meddle with, to interfere, c. daṅ (dang) (= _dri-ba ('dri ba)); té-mkan (the mkhan) belonging together, c. la, belonging to a thing; *ma-té-a* (*ma the a*) W. for ma-té-bar (ma the bar), = ma-ytógs-par (ma gtogs par); te-rég (the reg) the connexion or relation of ownership, _di-la yáb-kyi te-rég med ('di la yab kyi the reg med) to this my father has no claims Mil. nt.

col. té-tse (the tse), diminutive teu (the'u), resp. pyag-té (phyag the) seal, signet, stamp, *té-tse gyáb-ce* (*the tse rgyab ce*), or *nán-ce* (*nan ce*) to seal, to stamp; *sa-té* (*sa the*) Tar. 79, 12(?); *té-tse lag-kór táṅ-wa* (*the tse lag 'khor btang ba*) to engage, to bind one's self by a seal in some common concern.

doubt, scruple, uncertainty, perplexity, te-tsóm skyes (the tshom skyes), byed (byed) (W. *čo* (*co*)), za (za), te-tsóm-du gyur (the tshom du gyur) I am doubtful; te-tsom za-ba-rnams (the tshom za ba rnams) scrupulous, irresolute persons Pth.; *te-tsom maṅ-po rag* (*the tshom mang po rag*) W. I am in great perplexity, I am quite at a loss; te-tsóm źig _dri-ba (the tshom zhig 'dri ba) to utter a doubt Dzl.
col. straight, upright, firm; smooth, without folds or wrinkles; te-ré tiṅ (the re ting) C. draw (the carpet) smooth.
W. incomplete, defective, unfinished, te-rél-la lus soṅ (the rel la lus song) (the loaf) is not whole, there has already been cut from it.

I. sbst. 1. vehicle, carriage, riding-beast, rtaí tég-pa-la źon (rta'i theg pa la zhon) he mounted on horseback Dzl.; tég-pa lṅa-brgyá bśams (theg pa lnga brgya bshams) he procured five hundred conveyances (horses, elephants, carriages) Dzl. 2. for attaining to salvation, tég-pa ysum (theg pa gsum) three conveyances are generally mentioned, but in most cases only two are specified, viz. tég-(-pa) dman(-pa) (theg (-pa) dman (-pa)), हीनयान, and teg(-pa) čén-po (theg (-pa) chen po), महायान, gen. called 'the little and the great conveyance or vehicle', by means of which the distant shore of salvation may be reached. Yet mention is also made of a sṅágs-kyi tég-pa (sngags kyi theg pa), मन्त्रयान mantrayāna, e.g. Tar. 180, 13. For more particulars about these vehicles, and other more or less confused and contradictory notions, the works of Köppen and esp. Wasiljew may be consulted. II. vb. 1. to lift, raise, hold up, support Mil., Glr.; hence kri-tégs (khri thegs) leg of a table Sch.; teg-kúg (theg khug) C. knapsack, travelling-bag. — 2. to raise, set up fig. bśad-gád (bshad gad) to raise a loud laugh Mil. — 3. most. frq. to be able to carry, ji tég-pa (ji theg pa) as much as you are able to carry Dzl.; mis teg-tsád ycig (mis theg tshad gcig) as much as one man is able to carry Tar.; esp. with a negative: ma teg (ma theg) he was not able to hold him up Dzl.; mi-teg kur (mi theg khur) to carry what is too heavy to be carried (by ordinary muscular strength), to strain one's self by lifting, Med.; to endure, tolerate, stand, Kóṅ-rnams-kyi nan ma teg-par (khong rnams kyi nan ma theg par) not being able to stand their urgent demands Mil.; to bear, to undergo without detriment, skyid teg sdug teg (skyid theg sdug theg) to be able to bear good fortune and ill fortune. Cf. _tégs-pa ('thegs pa), _dégs-pa ('degs pa). —

1. teṅ-ró (theng ro) Mil., *śa-téṅ* (*sha theng*) Ld., the dead body of an animal killed by beasts of prey. — 2. *téṅ-la* (*theng la*) C. down, downward, e.g. *kyúr-wa* (*skyur ba*), *yúg-pa* (*dbyug pa*), *bór-wa* (*'bor ba*), to fling down.
Pth., téṅ-bu (theng bu) Sch., *téṅ-kań* (*theng rkang*) W., lame, hobbling, limping.
time, times, teṅs lṅa (thengs lnga) five times Pth.; dbugs-teṅs ycig-la (dbugs thengs gcig la) in one breathing, at a stretch; without intermission Pth.
1. a little while, a moment. — 2. v. _ten-pa ('then pa).
